title: Taiwan 8-Day Itinerary
summary: Taipei-base itinerary with city temples, north-coast routes, alternate day plans, and the graduation weekend kept intact.

stay.label: No. 250, Wenhua 1st Rd, Guishan District
stay.address: No. 250, Wenhua 1st Rd, Guishan District, Taoyuan City, Taiwan 333
stay.note: Use this as the start and end point for the daily routes below.
stay.transit: Best rail access is Taoyuan Airport MRT A7 National Taiwan Sport University. A8 Chang Gung Memorial Hospital also works if you prefer a taxi pickup.

image_queries: Taipei 101 night skyline from Elephant Mountain

days.label: Day 1
days.focus: Arrival, Ximending warm-up, then an Xinyi skyline evening.
days.transport_note: Keep this first day compact. Ximending works well after hotel check-in, and the evening stays in the Xinyi pocket.

start_transfer.title: TPE Airport to Stay Base
start_transfer.instructions: After landing at Taoyuan International Airport, take the Airport MRT toward Taipei and get off at A7 National Taiwan Sport University Station. From there, walk or take a short taxi along Wenhua 1st Rd to No. 250.
start_transfer.note: If you have heavy luggage, go straight by taxi from the airport instead.

places.name: Ximending
places.summary: Easy first-city stop for a light wander, snacks, and a low-effort reset after arrival and hotel check-in.
highlights: Walking streets and pop culture shops
places.transport: Take the MRT Blue or Green Line to Ximen Station and use Exit 6 for the central walking area.

foods.name: Ay-Chung Flour-Rice Noodles
foods.description: Thin vermicelli soup sold as a fast standing snack.

foods.name: Brown Sugar Boba Milk
foods.description: Fresh milk with warm brown-sugar pearls.

images.title: File:Pearl Milk Tea in Chun Shui Tang.jpg

places.name: Elephant Mountain Trail
places.summary: Short but stair-heavy climb for the classic Taipei 101 night skyline once the city lights come on.
highlights: Taipei 101 framed against the night skyline
places.transport: Walk 15 to 20 minutes from Taipei 101, or take a quick ride to Xiangshan Station and follow the signed trailhead near Lingyun Temple.
places.access_note: This is the best night-view add-on in the plan, but only if arrival energy is decent. The hike is short, popular, and mostly stairs, so expect a sweaty climb in late-May humidity.

places.name: Taipei 101 Observatory
places.summary: Finish in the Xinyi core after the hike for a clean skyline-and-city-lights pairing.
places.transport: Take the MRT Red Line to Taipei 101/World Trade Center Station and follow the mall signs to the observatory entrance.

transfers.instructions: From Ximending, ride east toward Xiangshan or take a direct taxi to the Elephant Mountain trailhead in Xinyi.
transfers.note: Taxi is the better move if arrival fatigue is still high.

transfers.instructions: After the climb, head back down toward Taipei 101 for the final skyline stop and the rest of the Xinyi area.
transfers.note: This is the most natural sequence for the evening because the two stops sit in the same district.

days.label: Day 2
days.focus: Northeast rail-and-taxi loop with cats, waterfall, trail time, and Jiufen after dark.
days.transport_note: Use TRA plus taxi hops, or take a driver if you want the cleanest Teapot Mountain timing. Jiufen is the lantern-view finish.

start_transfer.title: Stay Base to Houtong Cat Village
start_transfer.mode: Airport MRT + TRA via Ruifang or direct driver
start_transfer.instructions: Take the Airport MRT to Taipei Main Station, transfer to the TRA toward Ruifang, then continue one stop to Houtong. A direct driver is faster if you want more time at Teapot Mountain.
start_transfer.note: Train works for the first half well, but the trail and Jiufen finish are easier with taxis once you reach the Ruifang area.

places.name: Houtong Cat Village
places.summary: Former mining village turned cat-friendly stop with station-side lanes, footbridges, and resident cats everywhere.
highlights: Easy photo stops with village cats
places.transport: Best reached by taxi or driver from Shifen, or by train via Ruifang if you want to stay on rail. The cat village begins right around Houtong Station.

places.name: Shifen Waterfall
places.summary: Broad waterfall reached by paved paths and bridge crossings from the visitor side.
places.transport: From Ruifang or Jiufen, continue by train or car toward Shifen. Walk from the visitor area to the falls.

images.title: File:TRA DRC1029 and Pingxi Sky Lanterns at Shifen Old Street 20171028.jpg

places.name: Shifen Old Street Walk
places.summary: Railway-side old street with lantern shops, snack stops, and an easy walk that pairs naturally with the waterfall.
places.transport: Continue from the waterfall visitor area by foot, taxi, or a quick local hop into central Shifen.

places.name: Teapot Mountain Trail
places.summary: Classic Jinguashi ridge hike with exposed steps, sea views, and the iconic teapot-shaped summit rock.
places.transport: The cleanest link from Shifen or Jiufen is by taxi or driver to the Jinguashi trailhead area.
places.access_note: This is the most weather-sensitive add-on in the revised plan. Skip or shorten it if rain, fog, or trail fatigue sets in after the Houtong and Shifen loop.

places.name: Jiufen Old Street
places.summary: Lantern alleys, tea houses, and the classic night atmosphere once the hills start glowing after dark.
places.transport: Reach Jiufen by bus, taxi, or driver from Ruifang or Shifen. Expect stairs and uphill lanes.

foods.name: Taro Balls
foods.description: Chewy taro and sweet potato balls served hot or cold.
foods.where: Dessert shops on Jiufen Old Street

manual_images.title: File:Taro Balls at Jiufen, New Taipei City, Sept 2011.jpg

images.title: File:Taro Balls at Jiufen, New Taipei City, Sept 2011.jpg

foods.name: Peanut Ice Cream Roll
foods.description: Thin wrapper with shaved peanut brittle, ice cream, and cilantro.

manual_images.title: File:A-Zhu Peanut Ice Cream Roll, Jiufen Old Street, 2024.jpg

images.title: File:A-Zhu Peanut Ice Cream Roll, Jiufen Old Street, 2024.jpg

transfers.instructions: From Houtong, continue toward Shifen by taxi for the fastest link, or reverse to Ruifang and connect to the Pingxi Line.
transfers.note: Taxi saves a transfer and protects the rest of the day.

transfers.instructions: After the waterfall, move into central Shifen for the old street walk and snack stretch before leaving the Pingxi side.
transfers.note: This is the easiest same-area add-on of the full day.

transfers.instructions: Continue south toward Jinguashi for the Teapot Mountain trailhead. This leg is not worth forcing on public transport if you want to keep Jiufen for evening.
transfers.note: Expect roughly 30 to 40 minutes depending on traffic and trailhead choice.

transfers.instructions: Descend from the trail and continue a short distance into Jiufen Old Street for the lantern-view evening finish.
transfers.note: This is a short last leg, which is why the Teapot Mountain pairing works.

days.label: Day 3
days.focus: Temple-heavy Taipei day that shifts north for Tamsui sunset before returning to Dadaocheng.
days.transport_note: MRT covers the city temples well. Use taxis selectively for the Guandu-Tianyuan-Tamsui cluster if you do not want to rush the sunset.

start_transfer.title: Stay Base to Lungshan Temple
start_transfer.instructions: Take the Airport MRT to Taipei Main Station, then switch to the Blue Line for Longshan Temple Station Exit 1.
start_transfer.note: Leave early. This is still the longest pure rail start among the central Taipei days.

places.name: Lungshan Temple
places.summary: Historic temple with carved roofs, incense, and active worship throughout the day.
places.transport: Take the MRT Blue Line to Longshan Temple Station, Exit 1. The temple is directly outside.

places.name: Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall
places.summary: Large memorial plaza with formal gardens and the hourly guard ceremony.
places.transport: Take the MRT Red or Green Line to Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall Station, Exit 5.

places.name: Guandu Temple
places.summary: River-facing temple complex with tunnels, roof terraces, and long-standing Mazu worship.
places.transport: Take the MRT Red Line to Guandu Station, then bus or taxi to the temple entrance.

places.name: Tianyuan Temple
places.summary: Circular multi-tier temple complex known for blossom season and broad open grounds.
places.transport: Taxi or local bus from Tamsui area. This stop is easier by car than by full MRT-only routing.

manual_images.title: File:Tiansheng Gate, Tamsui Wuji Tianyuan Temple 20230323.jpg

images.title: File:Tiansheng Gate, Tamsui Wuji Tianyuan Temple 20230323.jpg

places.name: Tamsui Sunset Walk
places.summary: Riverside promenade finish with ferries, broad estuary views, and the easiest sunset stop on the north side of Taipei.
places.transport: Continue by taxi from Tianyuan Temple or return to the Red Line and ride all the way north to Tamsui for the waterfront.

places.name: Dihua Street
places.summary: End back in Dadaocheng for an evening walk through the restored shophouse stretch once the riverfront light is gone.
places.transport: Take the MRT to Beimen or Daqiaotou and walk into the Dadaocheng area.

foods.name: Taiwan Oolong Tea
foods.description: Good stop for tea tasting and easy gifts.
foods.where: Traditional tea shops on Dihua Street

transfers.instructions: Walk back to Longshan Temple Station, ride east to Ximen, then switch to the Green Line for Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall Station.
transfers.note: This is a straightforward central Taipei hop and keeps the morning clean.

transfers.instructions: From Liberty Square, connect back through Taipei Main and continue north on the Red Line toward Guandu, or take a taxi to save time before the outer-city temple cluster.
transfers.note: Taxi is more efficient if the memorial hall runs long.

transfers.instructions: Continue from Guandu Temple to Tianyuan Temple by taxi for the simplest same-direction northbound move.
transfers.note: This is the most time-sensitive internal transfer of the day.

transfers.instructions: Go onward into Tamsui for the sunset waterfront rather than backtracking into central Taipei first.
transfers.note: The sunset stop works best if you commit to the northbound sequence.

transfers.instructions: After sunset, ride the Red Line back toward central Taipei and walk into Dihua Street from Daqiaotou or Beimen.
transfers.note: This gives you a softer city finish than another night market.

days.label: Day 4
days.focus: North coast scenery day with one trail segment before the beach and final-night market run.
days.transport_note: Use a driver for this route. Yehliu, Bitou, and Baishawan sit on different parts of the coast, and public transport turns this into a very long day.

start_transfer.title: Stay Base to Yehliu Geopark
start_transfer.instructions: Leave Guishan by car and go directly to Yehliu Geopark for the morning coastal stop.
start_transfer.note: Early arrival keeps the rock formations far more comfortable.

places.name: Yehliu Geopark
places.summary: Coastal rock park with sea-eroded formations and open ocean exposure.
places.transport: Driver or bus from Taipei to Yehliu Geopark. Enter early to avoid the mid-morning crowd.

places.name: Bitou Cape Trail
places.summary: Sea-cliff boardwalk and headland trail with strong Pacific exposure and one of the cleanest coastal walks near Taipei.
places.transport: Best reached by driver from Yehliu. A bus connection exists but stretches the timing too much for the full day.

places.name: Baishawan Beach
places.summary: Short beach reset before heading back south into Taipei for the night-market finish.
places.transport: Continue by car from Tamsui or Tianyuan. Public transport is possible but slower and more fragmented.

places.name: Shilin Night Market
places.summary: Large final-night market with heavier crowd volume and a wider snack mix than Raohe.
places.transport: Take the MRT Red Line to Jiantan Station and walk south into the market area.

foods.name: Oyster Omelet
foods.description: Egg, starch batter, oysters, and sweet-savory sauce.

foods.name: XXL Fried Chicken Cutlet
foods.description: Large flat fried cutlet with pepper and chili seasoning.

manual_images.title: File:Hot-Star fried-chicken cutlet at Zhongli Night Market.jpg

images.title: File:Hot-Star fried-chicken cutlet at Zhongli Night Market.jpg

foods.name: Stinky Tofu
foods.description: Fermented tofu, deep-fried and served with pickles.

manual_images.title: File:Stinky tofu at the Xincheng Night Market.jpg

images.title: File:Stinky tofu at the Xincheng Night Market.jpg

transfers.instructions: Continue east along the coast from Yehliu to Bitou for the trail portion of the day.
transfers.note: This is the longest scenic road leg of the route and is much easier with your own transport.

transfers.instructions: After the Bitou walk, cross back toward Baishawan for a lower-effort beach stop before returning into Taipei.
transfers.note: This is the biggest backtrack in the revised itinerary, so keep trail time realistic.

transfers.instructions: Leave the beach and head back into Taipei for Shilin Night Market. Taxi is simplest; MRT works if you re-enter near the Red Line.
transfers.note: Try to be in Taipei before the late-night market surge.

days.label: Day 5
days.focus: Choose one area plan and stay committed to it for the full daytime block before ending at Raohe.
days.transport_note: The two options are deliberately separated by zone. Do not mix them on the same day unless you want to burn a lot of time in transit.

plans.label: Plan 1
plans.title: Yangmingshan + Raohe Night Market
plans.focus: Qingtiangang in the morning, more Yangmingshan scenery in the afternoon, then back into the city for Raohe.
plans.transport_note: This plan works by taxi plus park shuttle, or by a bus-heavy Yangmingshan day if you start early.

start_transfer.title: Stay Base to Qingtiangang
start_transfer.instructions: Go north from Guishan toward Yangmingshan and start directly at Qingtiangang for the cleanest morning walk.
start_transfer.note: A taxi start is the easiest way to protect time inside the park itself.

places.name: Qingtiangang Grassland
places.summary: High grassland plateau in Yangmingshan with an easy loop, open skyline, and a very different feel from the Taipei city days.
places.transport: Use a taxi or Yangmingshan shuttle and start directly at Qingtiangang to keep the first half of the day compact.

places.name: Yangmingshan Scenic Loop
places.summary: Flexible second-half continuation for nearby lookouts, short walks, and volcanic park scenery without leaving the Yangmingshan zone.
places.transport: Stay inside the park by shuttle, bus, or taxi rather than dropping back into the city before evening.

places.name: Raohe Night Market
places.summary: Compact night market with strong street-food lineup and a straightforward walking route.
places.transport: Take the MRT Green Line to Songshan Station, Exit 5. The market starts right beside Ciyou Temple.

foods.name: Fuzhou Black Pepper Bun
foods.description: Clay-oven bun with pork, scallion, and black pepper.
image_queries: Pepper bun Raohe Street Night Market

images.title: File:Pepper bun at Raohe Street Night Market 20070622.jpg

foods.name: Sweet Potato Balls
foods.description: Fried, hollow, and chewy snack sold by the bag.
image_queries: sweet potato balls Taiwan street food

images.title: File:2011-10-10 Street vendors making sweets at a shop in Jiufen Old Street.jpg
File:East Entrance of Raohe Street Night Market 20060118 night.jpg

transfers.instructions: Stay inside the Yangmingshan park zone and continue to the next scenic stop rather than returning downhill too early.
transfers.note: This keeps the full afternoon in the same area, matching the photo plan.

transfers.instructions: Head back into Taipei in the late afternoon and finish at Raohe Night Market beside Songshan Station.
transfers.note: Leaving the mountain area before the evening commute keeps this manageable.

plans.label: Plan 2
plans.title: Crocodile Island + Raohe Night Market
plans.focus: Reservoir-view morning around Crocodile Island, more Shiding-area viewpoints after lunch, then Raohe at night.
plans.transport_note: Treat this as a driver day. The whole point is to stay in the Shiding reservoir area until the evening return.

start_transfer.title: Stay Base to Crocodile Island Viewpoint
start_transfer.instructions: Leave directly from Guishan by car and head into the Shiding reservoir roads for the Crocodile Island viewpoint first.
start_transfer.note: This route is not efficient by public transport.

places.name: Crocodile Island Viewpoint
places.summary: Reservoir viewpoint over the peninsula shape that gives the site its nickname.
places.transport: Drive to the roadside pull-off near the viewpoint trailhead. Final access is on foot over a short but steep path.

places.name: Thousand-Island Lake
places.summary: Series of reservoir inlets framed by tea fields and layered ridgelines.
places.transport: Continue by car from the Crocodile Island area; viewpoints are spread across the ridge roads.

places.name: Raohe Night Market
places.summary: Compact night market with strong street-food lineup and a straightforward walking route.
places.transport: Take the MRT Green Line to Songshan Station, Exit 5. The market starts right beside Ciyou Temple.

foods.name: Fuzhou Black Pepper Bun
foods.description: Clay-oven bun with pork, scallion, and black pepper.
image_queries: Pepper bun Raohe Street Night Market

images.title: File:Pepper bun at Raohe Street Night Market 20070622.jpg

foods.name: Sweet Potato Balls
foods.description: Fried, hollow, and chewy snack sold by the bag.
image_queries: sweet potato balls Taiwan street food

images.title: File:2011-10-10 Street vendors making sweets at a shop in Jiufen Old Street.jpg
File:East Entrance of Raohe Street Night Market 20060118 night.jpg

transfers.instructions: Stay on the Shiding ridge roads and continue to another reservoir overlook for the same-area afternoon exploration.
transfers.note: This mirrors the “continue same area exploration” note in the image.

transfers.mode: Car back to Taipei + MRT or taxi
transfers.instructions: Return to Taipei after the reservoir stops and finish with dinner at Raohe Night Market.
transfers.note: Raohe is the cleanest market finish because it is easy to re-enter from the east side.

days.label: Day 6
days.focus: Two different final full-day options: Shiding reservoir country with Dihua, or Yilan coast with an Xinyi city finish.
days.transport_note: Pick one plan early. The reservoir plan and the Yilan plan are not interchangeable once you leave in the morning.

plans.label: Plan 1
plans.title: Crocodile Island + Shiding + Dihua Street
plans.focus: Reservoir lookouts in the morning, Shiding old street and tea-country walking later, then Dihua Street at night.
plans.transport_note: Best done with a driver or rental car all day, then a simple city return in the evening.

start_transfer.title: Stay Base to Crocodile Island and Shiding
start_transfer.instructions: Leave from Guishan by car and go straight into the Shiding reservoir area for the viewpoint sequence before village stops later on.
start_transfer.note: This keeps the day geographically tight until the evening return to Taipei.

places.name: Crocodile Island Viewpoint
places.summary: Reservoir viewpoint over the peninsula shape that gives the site its nickname.
places.transport: Drive to the roadside pull-off near the viewpoint trailhead. Final access is on foot over a short but steep path.

places.name: Thousand-Island Lake
places.summary: Series of reservoir inlets framed by tea fields and layered ridgelines.
places.transport: Continue by car from the Crocodile Island area; viewpoints are spread across the ridge roads.

places.name: Shiding Old Street
places.summary: Short old street with stilted riverside buildings and tofu-focused snack shops.
places.transport: Drive back down into central Shiding and park before walking the old street.

foods.name: Tofu Ice Cream
foods.description: Cold tofu-based dessert sold by local tofu shops.

images.title: File:Shiding River Walking Trail 石碇溪步道 - panoramio.jpg

places.name: Shiding Tea Walk
places.summary: Short tea-country walk that keeps you in the reservoir area after the viewpoints before heading back toward Taipei.
places.transport: Continue by car from the Crocodile Island and Shiding lookouts and park near the local trail access.

manual_images.title: File:Shiding River Walking Trail 石碇溪步道 - panoramio.jpg

places.name: Dihua Street
places.summary: Historic trading street for tea, dried goods, fabric stores, and restored shophouses.
places.transport: Take the MRT to Beimen or Daqiaotou and walk into the Dadaocheng area.

foods.name: Taiwan Oolong Tea
foods.description: Good stop for tea tasting and easy gifts.
foods.where: Traditional tea shops on Dihua Street

transfers.instructions: Continue deeper through the same reservoir roads for the broader Shiding morning block.
transfers.note: This is the cleanest reading of “Crocodile Island + Shiding” from the photo.

transfers.instructions: Drop down into central Shiding and park for the old street stretch after the reservoir views.
transfers.note: Lunch works naturally around the old street stop.

transfers.instructions: Add a tea-country walk nearby before committing to the evening return toward Taipei.
transfers.note: This is the lighter outdoor piece of the afternoon.

transfers.instructions: Return to the city and finish with an evening walk through Dihua Street in Dadaocheng.
transfers.note: Dihua is the calmer finish for the Shiding version of the day.

plans.label: Plan 2
plans.title: Yilan + Waiao Beach + Xinyi Area
plans.focus: Wufengchi Waterfall in the morning, Waiao Beach in the afternoon, then back to Taipei for the Xinyi district at night.
plans.transport_note: Driver is easiest for the waterfall-to-beach combo. Train plus taxi also works if you start early from the base.

start_transfer.title: Stay Base to Wufengchi Waterfall
start_transfer.mode: Driver or Airport MRT + TRA via Taipei Main
start_transfer.instructions: Go east toward Jiaoxi for the waterfall start. A driver keeps the day smooth, while rail requires a Taipei Main transfer and short taxi from Jiaoxi.
start_transfer.note: Do not start too late if you want the beach and Taipei return on the same day.

images.title: File:Yilan Wufengqi Waterfall-宜蘭-五峰旗瀑布.jpg

places.name: Wufengchi Waterfall
places.summary: Layered waterfall walk near Jiaoxi with humid forest paths and a straightforward nature stop outside Taipei.
places.transport: Fastest approach is by driver, but train to Jiaoxi plus a short taxi also works if you leave early.

places.name: Waiao Beach
places.summary: Relaxed Yilan coast stop with surf, open beach views, and Guishan Island offshore on a clear day.
highlights: Guishan Island view on clear weather
places.transport: Continue by taxi or driver from Jiaoxi or Wufengchi. Rail is possible via Wai'ao Station if needed.

places.name: Xinyi District Night Walk
places.summary: Flexible city finish around Taipei 101 with lit boulevards, department-store frontage, and easy dinner options.
places.transport: Take the MRT to Taipei 101/World Trade Center Station and stay on foot within the Xinyi core.

transfers.instructions: Continue from Wufengchi toward the coast for a lighter beach stretch at Waiao.
transfers.note: This is a straightforward same-county transfer and works well after the waterfall walk.

transfers.instructions: Head back to Taipei in the late afternoon and finish the evening around the Xinyi district and Taipei 101 area.
transfers.note: Xinyi is the cleaner city finish for the Yilan version of the day.

gallery.alt: Fucha Graduation and Maus Birthday celebration

days.label: Day 8
days.transport_note: Plan an early airport transfer from central Taipei to TPE.

start_transfer.title: Stay Base to TPE Airport
start_transfer.instructions: For the 09:35 departure, the cleanest move is an early taxi straight to Taoyuan International Airport. If you prefer rail, take the Airport MRT from A7 National Taiwan Sport University toward the airport.
start_transfer.note: Leave with extra margin for check-in and baggage drop.
